author: pangy
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Database views for notes with table, board, gallery, formulas, filters, and inline editing.

name: Note Database

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
1.1.1 Hotfix release

- Show only saved computed frontmatter properties that actually exist in the current database scope, and allow clearing multiple saved formula properties at once.
- Clean up the original frontmatter property when converting a normal property to a supported file.* metadata field.
- Prevent virtual file.* metadata fields, such as file.backlinks, from being written as empty frontmatter values on new notes or schema changes.
- Fix text equality filters so unrelated text values without digits are no longer treated as equal.
- Keep the chart summary row below the chart after chart option changes or chart rebuilds.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Note Database 1.1.0 adds a full chart view while keeping the plugin focused on local Markdown and frontmatter workflows.

### Added

- Chart view with bar, horizontal bar, line, area, donut, number, stacked, grouped, percent-stacked, and mixed chart layouts.
- Chart configuration popover for grouping, values, aggregation, sorting, visible groups, color palettes, style options, data labels, reference lines, and export.
- Drilldown modal for chart marks, so users can inspect matching records before applying a filter.
- Flexible summary bar calculations for numeric, date, checkbox, unique, empty, and filled values.
- Database-file tab controls for users who want database files to always open in a new tab, avoid duplicate tabs for the same database file, or combine both behaviors.
- Computed frontmatter cleanup action for removing saved computed properties from notes in the current database scope.

### Improved

- Embedded database views can hide the database header and preserve more space for content.
- Opening database files from the dashboard, file explorer, and drag/open fallback paths now follows the same tab strategy.
- Shared dropdown popovers now make filters, sorting, source rules, settings, chart options, and status controls feel more consistent.
- `file.*` metadata fields are handled as file metadata instead of ordinary frontmatter properties.
- Option editing, drag feedback, chart labels, chart palettes, reference lines, board sticky headers, and long labels received final polish.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.0.9

- Fixed Obsidian review findings discovered after 1.0.8, including review-safe property icon rendering, computed checkbox styling and editor behavior, Obsidian API compatibility warnings, popout-window DOM guards, modal confirmation handling, Promise handling, broad type-safety cleanup, duplicate database-file tab regression from the audit-fix pass, and the final ZIP export buffer warning.

## 1.0.8 
- **Complete `.base` source rule import**: method chains (`file.inFolder("X").and(file.hasTag("#Y"))`), AND/OR/NOT logic, comparisons, type checks, regex, prefix/suffix, empty/truthy, negation, link/tag/property checks, multi-tag filters, and view-level filters are all converted. Complex expressions that cannot be mapped to structured rules are preserved as editable expression rules.
- **`.base` formula import with native syntax**: imported formulas retain Bases syntax and are evaluated by a dedicated engine supporting method chains, conditionals, date arithmetic, duration math, link resolution, string/number/list/object helpers, and summary aggregates (sum/count/average/unique/join). Cross-formula dependencies are resolved in definition order.
- **Computed field sync modes**: each database can now choose between display-only (virtual values, never written to frontmatter), manual (write back only when you click the sync button), and automatic (write back whenever values change). The toolbar shows a sync button in manual mode and a live indicator in automatic mode.
- **Smarter source rule editor**: the field picker groups options into note properties, formula fields, and file properties. The operator dropdown now filters by field type — number fields no longer show `in folder`, tag fields no longer show `starts with`, and so on. Legacy operators in saved rules are preserved in a compatibility group.
- **Bug fixes**: data safety, computed field sync, view state persistence, undo/redo reliability, embedded view permissions, CSV import/export, database deletion, property rename migration, and `.base` conversion accuracy.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix old frontmatter cleanup on property rename

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- **Markdown database files as the single storage model**: every database is now stored as a normal `db_view: true` Markdown file. Existing settings-based databases are migrated automatically when the plugin loads.
- **Manual ordering across all four views**: table rows, board cards, gallery cards, and list rows now share a LexoRank-like view order. Desktop users can drag items precisely into place, while explicit property sorting still takes precedence.
- **Touch-friendly manual ordering**: phone layouts expose move menus instead of relying on free-form drag. Table, gallery, and list views support up/down/top/bottom and cross-group moves; board cards can also move before or after a specific card.
- **More precise computed-field sync**: computed values update on relevant save events instead of depending on periodic full refreshes, reducing unnecessary work.
- **Faster inline editing**: ordinary property edits and card checkboxes use optimistic local updates where possible, reducing full-view flicker while values are written back to Markdown frontmatter.
- **Better database creation flow**: creating a database can scan existing frontmatter and open a property confirmation dialog. Newly created databases open immediately.
- **Refined grouping and file navigation**: custom group order is now an immediately saved popover; database files receive a `DB` marker in the file explorer; file-name fields reveal folder prefixes on hover.
- **Interaction polish**: date editing uses the same contextual popover language as text and number fields, horizontal scrolling is easier to discover, and wide lists reveal newly created entries from their leading edge.
- **Upgrade note**: after upgrading, please confirm that migrated database files, views, and embedded blocks open normally.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- **Mobile adaptation**: the toolbar, views, property panels, cell editing, and formula editor are now fully usable on phones and tablets.
- **Mobile-friendly reordering**: drag-only lists (property columns, sort rules, database lists, status options, cell options, group order) now include up/down move buttons for touch, while drag handles are hidden on mobile.
- **Mobile table selection**: Dashboard and embedded tables use tap-to-select mode on mobile — tap a cell to set the anchor, tap another to extend the selection; row checkboxes use the last-selected row as the range anchor.
- **Mobile card move**: board cards get a touch-friendly move menu (top/bottom, before/after another card, move to another group/subgroup) instead of drag-and-drop.
- **Disabled unreliable mobile drag**: column-header drag sort, column resize, row group drag, cell fill handle, board column drag/resize, and gallery width drag are hidden on mobile.
- **Mobile toolbar**: search box moves to the view-tab row; toolbar buttons wrap to a second scrollable row; view tabs disable drag reorder and use a menu with move-to-top/move-up/move-down/move-to-bottom.
- **Mobile formula editor**: inline autocomplete popup is disabled on mobile to avoid keyboard overlap; the bottom field/function panel remains available for search and tap-to-insert.
- **Refined cell text editing**: on desktop, text cell editing is now a contextual floating layer that appears at the cell position, instead of a modal card with title bar and Cancel/Save buttons.
- **Improved adaptive column width**: auto-fit now uses `canvas.measureText()` for lightweight measurement, with separate estimates for headers, text, and option badges — producing more compact and accurate results.
- **Visual polish**: cell option popovers use flat, pill-shaped items with better spacing; toolbar search focus no longer shows a highlighted background block; database description editor auto-grows with content height.
- **Bug fixes**: mobile floating editor positioning; property edit window element misalignment; formula error preview duplication.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new (vs 1.0.1)

- More reliable frontmatter writes and refresh
- Dedicated database-file view for database files (db_view: true)
- Embedded view improvements (cell range selection + CSV + Markdown ZIP export)
- Batch editing with undo and skipped-field notices
- Formula editor responsiveness and preview stability
- New setting: dashboard start page (settings DB vs database file)

Minimum Obsidian version: 1.4.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Note Database 1.0.1 is a maintenance release focused on table stability, formula editing polish, column management, and release audit cleanup.

## Highlights
- Added a GitHub Actions release workflow for tagged releases, including artifact provenance attestations for `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.
- Improved formula editor usability with stable autocomplete navigation, field option badges that match database option colors, richer AI prompt copying, and a steadier preview area.
- Improved table column handling, including fixed row-selection checkbox width, safer column resizing, synchronized grouped-table resize behavior, and auto-fit actions for one column or all visible columns.

## Fixes and polish
- Unified sorting state around `sortRules`, so toolbar badges, column headers, and sort popovers stay in sync.
- Column header sorting now cycles through ascending, descending, and no sort; column menus also include clear sort.
- Prevented column resize from accidentally triggering column sort.
- Fixed stale popovers when switching databases or views.
- Fixed deleted columns lingering in the UI by cleaning view references and refreshing the schema immediately after confirmation.
- Fixed formula sync so computed fields are synced across the full database source instead of only currently filtered rows.
- Fixed table header tooltips, empty/checkbox cell tooltips, sort indicator visibility, narrow column behavior, and drag target feedback.
- Fixed board option badges inheriting muted label color in some cases.
- Fixed inline option deletion hover styling and kept option colors consistent across table, board, gallery, list, presets, and formula details.
- Improved `.base` and CSV import confirmation by allowing display names to be reviewed or edited.

## Release quality
- Removed release audit warnings for `builtin-modules`, `!important`, duplicate selectors, and `:has()` usage.
- No data migration is required. Existing databases should continue to work with the same Markdown/frontmatter format.

releases.author: pangy9
releases.description:
Initial public release of Note Database.
**Highlights**:
- Table, board, gallery, and list views for Markdown notes.
- Inline frontmatter editing, filters, sorting, grouping, and batch selection.
- Computed fields with formula editor, live preview, and calculation steps.
- Embedded read-only database views for Markdown notes.
- CSV + Markdown ZIP import/export and .base file conversion.
- Local-first operation with English, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese UI.
releases.name: Note Database 1.0.0
